metadata.title: Topological Constraints: ∫∫_Σ K DA + ∫_∂Σ κ_g Ds
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Topological Constraints: ∫∫_Σ K DA + ∫_∂Σ κ_g Ds
master_row.canonical_statement: The toroidal boundary imposes topological constraints through the Gauss-Bonnet theorem. For a closed 2-surface $\Sigma$ embedded in 3-space: \begin{equation} \int\int_\Sigma K\,dA + \int_{\partial\Sigma} \kappa_g\,ds = 2\pi\chi(\Sigma) \end{equation}
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Topological Constraints’.
master_row.dictionary: The toroidal boundary imposes topological constraints through the Gauss-Bonnet theorem.
master_row.encyclopedia: Topological Constraints: ∫∫_Σ K DA + ∫_∂Σ κ_g Ds is an integral expression, written ∫∫_Σ K dA + ∫_∂Σ κ_g ds = 2πχ(Σ). It is obtained by integrating over a domain, accumulating contributions across the region of interest. It is built from π, χ, Σ, defining ∫∫_Σ K dA + ∫_∂Σ κ_g ds. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
